assumptions: Each run uses a fresh room and canvas to avoid queue and document contention.
The benchmark uses the live viewer plus direct canvas runner path for deterministic screenshot capture.
The GPT-5.4 Low comparison label uses the official OpenAI GPT-5.4 runtime with a lean preset.
Token usage and estimated cost are recorded when the provider returns usage metadata.

pricing.notes: Anthropic Claude Platform list pricing for Haiku 4.5.

variants.label: GPT-5.4 Low
assumptions: This comparison slot uses the official OpenAI GPT-5.4 runtime with a lean benchmark preset.
Low-latency behavior is approximated through the precise preset and lean followup budget.

scenarios.label: Business Operating Plan
scenarios.description: A structured planning board with objectives, owners, and risks.

steps.label: Plan board
steps.message: Build a quarterly operating plan canvas for a startup launch. Use a clear title, three swimlanes for objectives, owners, and risks, and enough notes or cards that an operator could talk through it on a call.

scenarios.label: Podcast Episode Map
scenarios.description: An episode board with segment sequencing and sponsor placement.

steps.label: Episode outline
steps.message: Design a podcast episode planning board for a design-and-technology show. Map the cold open, segment beats, sponsor slot, listener questions, and closing. Make it readable for collaborators in a live canvas review.

scenarios.label: Collaborative Brainstorm
scenarios.description: A cluster-based ideation board with grouped themes.

steps.label: Theme clusters
steps.message: Create a collaborative brainstorm board for new realtime-canvas features. Show three thematic clusters, leave room for new notes, and label the clusters with short, memorable names.

scenarios.label: Scratchpad Systems Sketch
scenarios.description: An informal but legible systems sketch with arrows and annotations.

steps.label: Latency sketch
steps.message: On a blank board, sketch the low-latency canvas pipeline as a rough scratchpad: voice input, queue, steward, screenshot, action stream, and ack loop. Keep it legible but informal, like something a staff engineer would draw live.

scenarios.label: Presentation Storyboard
scenarios.description: A visually strong presentation board that continues over two turns.

steps.label: Opening slide
steps.message: Draft a presentation storyboard for a product kickoff. Create a strong opening panel, two supporting panels, and concise visual hierarchy for a live walkthrough.

steps.label: Continue storyboard
steps.message: Continue the existing storyboard without recreating the opening panel. Add a proof or evidence area and a final next-steps panel that fits the composition.

scenarios.label: Remote Board Game Night
scenarios.description: A playful board for turn order, score, and shared game state.

steps.label: Game state board
steps.message: Design a remote board game night canvas with a title, turn order area, score track, shared resource pool, and a playful mascot or flourish. It should feel useful, not childish.

scenarios.label: Collaborative Drawing Mural
scenarios.description: A freer canvas that pressures the pen and spatial-composition path.

steps.label: Draw mural
steps.message: Use the canvas like a collaborative drawing wall. Create a lively mural with at least some freehand energy, a central focal area, and supporting marks around it. Keep it tasteful and presentation-ready.

scenarios.label: Freehand Sketchnote Recap
scenarios.description: A live sketchnote board with handwriting energy, arrows, and loose grouping.

steps.label: Sketchnote recap
steps.message: Create a freehand sketchnote recap of a product strategy meeting. Use pen energy, arrows, boxed callouts, and short handwritten-feeling labels. It should look intentional, not sloppy.

scenarios.label: Freehand Mascot Poster
scenarios.description: A poster-like scene that pressures composition and expressive pen strokes.

steps.label: Mascot poster
steps.message: Use the canvas to create a freehand poster for a remote team offsite, with a mascot, headline, supporting notes, and hand-drawn accents. Favor expressive pen marks over rigid box layout.

scenarios.label: Freehand Journey Map
scenarios.description: A journey map that mixes looser drawing with structured storytelling.

steps.label: Journey map
steps.message: Draw a freehand customer journey map for onboarding into a realtime collaboration product. Use a left-to-right flow, handwritten-feeling labels, emotional highs and lows, and lightweight visual flourishes.

scenarios.label: Multi-Fairy Zones
scenarios.description: A board divided into clearly separated work zones to mimic swarm collaboration.

steps.label: Zone layout
steps.message: Lay out a canvas as if three fairies were collaborating in parallel. Create three clearly separated zones with distinct visual purposes and leave a shared center for synthesis.

scenarios.label: Meeting Retro Board
scenarios.description: A classic retro board with structure and enough density to test organization.

steps.label: Retro columns
steps.message: Create a team retrospective board with three columns for wins, friction, and experiments. Populate it with enough notes to feel real and keep the spacing disciplined.

scenarios.label: Call-Ready Hero Board
scenarios.description: A high-signal board optimized for being reviewed live on a video call.

steps.label: Hero board
steps.message: Create a call-ready hero board for a live video review. Make one dominant idea area, supporting notes, and enough whitespace that a presenter could quickly orient teammates in realtime.
